title: perityle cinerea
intro: Perityle cinerea, also known as the desert marigold or gray marigold, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat wounds, inflammation, and respiratory issues due to its high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids. The health benefits of Perityle cinerea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Perityle cinerea may include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and internally to support respiratory health.
